summary: EEM Phase B: Travel Settlement + GL Reconciliation Testing - CRITICAL NAVIGATION FAILURE PERSISTS. Backend 100% PASS (29/29 tests). Frontend CRITICAL FAILURE - Settlement module NOT accessible via URL navigation. Main agent's claim that navigation issue was 'RESOLVED' is INCORRECT.

critical_bugs.issue: URL query parameters (?portal=finance&module=fin-settlement-queue) are NOT parsed or used for navigation
critical_bugs.impact: BLOCKS Finance users from accessing Settlement module via direct URL. Module is completely inaccessible from frontend despite being registered in moduleRegistry.js and portalNav.js.
affected_routes: Finance portal → Kas & Pembayaran → Queue Settlement Dinas
critical_bugs.root_cause: App.js only handles hash-based navigation (#module-id) and localStorage state. URL query parameters are completely ignored. When user navigates to ?portal=X&module=Y, the app doesn't read these parameters and falls back to portal selector.
details: Module IS registered in moduleRegistry.js as 'fin-settlement-queue'
Module IS listed in portalNav.js under Finance → 'KAS & PEMBAYARAN' section
Direct URL navigation (?portal=finance&module=fin-settlement-queue) redirects to portal selector
App.js needs URLSearchParams parsing logic to read portal and module from query string
Current code only handles: hash navigation (#module-id), localStorage state, and manual portal selection
evidence: Playwright test shows portal selector page when navigating to ?portal=finance&module=fin-settlement-queue
No URLSearchParams parsing in App.js useEffect hooks
Page content shows 'Pilih Portal' instead of Settlement module UI

passed_tests: Backend: Submit and approve expense claim
Backend: Disburse expense claim with GL posting (JE-EXP-20260529-0005)
Backend: Submit and approve travel request
Backend: Mark cash advance as paid with GL JE (JE-ADV-20260529-0005)
Backend: Per diem configuration (list and update)
Backend: List settlements for travel request
Backend: Post GL settlement with correct journal entries (JE-STL-20260529-0003)
Backend: Outstanding advances report (2 items, Rp 2,000,000)
Backend: Journal entry verification (advance + settlement JEs created)
Backend: GL posting logic - Dr 6-3400 / Cr 1-1610 / Cr 1-1201 (additional scenario)
Frontend: Finance portal card visible in portal selector

success_percentage.frontend: 0% (module not accessible - URL query parameter routing broken)

items.task: Implement URL query parameter parsing in App.js
items.details: Add URLSearchParams parsing logic in App.js to read 'portal' and 'module' from query string (?portal=X&module=Y). Currently, the app only handles hash-based navigation (#module-id) and localStorage state. URL query parameters are completely ignored, causing navigation to fail.
implementation_guidance: Add useEffect hook in App.js to parse window.location.search using URLSearchParams
Extract 'portal' and 'module' parameters from query string
If both parameters are valid, call setSelectedPortal(portal) and setCurrentModule(module)
Ensure this runs after session restoration but before rendering portal selector
Example: const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search); const portal = params.get('portal'); const module = params.get('module');
verification: Verify Settlement module loads instead of portal selector
Verify sidebar shows 'Queue Settlement Dinas' menu item
Verify summary stats, tabs, and settlement table are visible

items.task: Test Settlement module UI after navigation fix
items.details: Once URL query parameter routing is fixed, verify all Settlement module features work correctly
test_scenarios: View outstanding advances report (should show 2 items)
View settlement list with existing data (3 posted settlements)
Verify reconciliation card shows correct amounts
Verify GL preview shows correct journal entries
Test tab navigation (Settlement Saya, Queue, Outstanding Advance)

tests: Get per diem calculation (Rp 350,000 x 4 days)

gl_posting_verification.status: CORRECT - Company pays additional Rp 500,000 to employee

phase_b_journal_entries.verification: Both JE numbers created successfully with correct GL accounts
